prerequisites: A Tempo account (Tempo for Jira Cloud) with permissions for the data you want to sync; the API returns only data your Tempo permissions grant access to.
Access to Tempo > Settings > Data Access > API integration to generate an API integration token.

Knowledge of the correct regional base path for your Tempo data: api.eu.tempo.io (Europe), api.us.tempo.io (Americas), or api.tempo.io (other or universal). Using a base path for a different cluster than where your data resides returns an authorization error.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: The connector authenticates with a single secret field, api_token. This is a Tempo API integration token (a Tempo OAuth 2.0 token) generated for an individual user under Tempo > Settings > Data Access > API integration. The token is sent on every request in the Authorization HTTP header as 'Authorization: Bearer <token>'.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Tempo API integration token
setupSteps.body: In Tempo, go to Tempo > Settings, scroll down to Data Access, and select API integration. Generate a token there. The token grants the same data access as your Tempo user permissions.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the required api_token config field to the Tempo API integration token you generated. This is the only field the source-tempo connector requires; it is stored as a secret.

setupSteps.title: Verify the credentials
setupSteps.body: Confirm the token works by calling a Tempo REST API endpoint with the Authorization header, for example: curl -v -H "Authorization: Bearer $token" "https://api.tempo.io/4/worklogs". Use the regional base path (api.eu.tempo.io, api.us.tempo.io, or api.tempo.io) that matches where your Tempo data resides, otherwise you receive an authorization error.
